claims.claim: The shadcn bento grid uses a six-cell layout with varied col-span and row-span values to create visual weight and hierarchy across cells.
claims.quote: a six-cell bento layout using varied col-span and row-span sizes to create visual weight and hierarchy

claims.claim: The bento grid is explicitly asymmetric — columns span different widths across cards — rather than a uniform grid.
claims.quote: an asymmetric bento grid hero with crosshair image placeholders in varied col-span cells

claims.claim: Larger cells are used to emphasize featured/primary content while smaller cells carry secondary information, establishing a deliberate visual hierarchy.
claims.quote: varied cell sizes deliberately create 'visual weight and hierarchy,' suggesting larger cells emphasize featured content while smaller cells support secondary information

claims.claim: The implementation relies on Tailwind CSS grid utilities (not a dedicated masonry library) combined with shadcn/ui, TypeScript, and motion/react staggered entrance animations.
claims.quote: Built with 'Tailwind CSS grid utilities,' 'shadcn/ui,' TypeScript, and 'motion/react staggered entrance animations.'

claims.claim: Each grid cell includes a small label overlay to guide content placement, indicating a slot-based template pattern rather than auto-flowing cards.
claims.quote: Each cell includes a small label overlay so designers know what content to place
